basics.name: Shobhit S. Chaturvedi
basics.label: Computational Chemist | Postdoctoral Scholar
basics.summary: Computational chemist investigating how protein scaffolds control enzyme catalysis — and translating these principles into enzyme engineering for health, energy, and sustainability. 7+ years of experience and 25+ publications combining multiscale simulations, machine learning, and quantum computing. Currently at ETH Zurich; previously at UCLA and Michigan Tech.

location.address: Department of Chemistry and Applied Biosciences

work.name: ETH Zurich
work.summary: Department of Chemistry and Applied Biosciences
highlights: Member of X-prize finalist team developing a high-performance pipeline for biological applications on Quantum Computers
Scaling Chemoton's capacity by integrating QM/MM support for automated reaction network exploration in enzyme catalysis
Collaborating with experimental teams to engineer Heme metalloenzyme P450 variants via Metal-Hydride Hydrogen Atom Transfer
Contributing to a multi-group initiative on catalytic mechanism and electronic structure of the Nitrogenase enzyme

work.name: University of California, Los Angeles
work.summary: Department of Chemistry and Biochemistry. Advisor: Dr. Anastassia N. Alexandrova
highlights: Pioneered 3D electric field mapping frameworks for enzyme catalysis
Developed ML models to predict enzyme function from electric field fingerprints (up to 84% accuracy)
Demonstrated electric field optimization through directed evolution of protoglobin
Automated Bash/Python/HPC pipelines for ~4,000 enzyme variants and ~20,000 MD simulations
Collaborated on drug discovery: FEP simulations and DFT for α-synuclein aggregation inhibitors
Mentored 6 researchers (4 graduate, 2 undergraduate), producing 3 co-authored publications
Secured >$232K in ACCESS computational grants as PI/Co-PI

work.name: Michigan Technological University
work.summary: Department of Chemistry. Advisor: Dr. Christo Z. Christov
highlights: QM/MM studies of non-heme iron enzyme mechanisms
Investigated second coordination sphere effects on catalysis
Published 15+ peer-reviewed papers during graduate studies
Mentored 3 PhD students and 5 undergraduates

work.name: Michigan Technological University
work.summary: Department of Chemistry. Courses: University Chemistry Lab I, Physical Chemistry Lab I & II

work.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay
work.summary: Department of Chemical Engineering. Advisor: Dr. Ateeque Malani
highlights: Molecular dynamics study of interaction between calcium oxalate and phytic acid — investigating mechanism of kidney stone formation

work.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay
work.summary: Department of Chemical Engineering. Advisor: Dr. Ateeque Malani
highlights: Research internship on 'Mechanism of Kidney Stone Formation'
Advisor: Dr. Christo Z. Christov
Dissertation: Multilevel Computational Investigation into the Dynamics and Reaction Mechanisms of Non-Heme Iron and 2-Oxoglutarate Dependent Enzymes
Priyadarshini Institute of Engineering & Technology, Nagpur
